patient.query: Yeah, it’s really tough. I feel like I’m just trying to do my best, you know? But when they ignore me, I get this sinking feeling. It’s like, am I not communicating well? Or am I just failing as a parent? It’s frustrating and pretty overwhelming sometimes.

am I just failing as a parent?

patient.query: Exactly. It’s just so disheartening when I feel like I’m putting in the effort but not getting through to them. I worry that I’m not giving them what they need, and that makes me feel... I don’t know, like I’m letting them down. I want them to feel heard and understood, but often it just feels like chaos.

But then these little moments of conflict just... spiral. It’s like I’m not the parent I imagined I’d be, and that scares me. I don't want them to feel neglected or anything like that.

I don't want them to feel neglected

patient.query: Yeah, it definitely weighs on me a lot. Sometimes, I get so stressed that I snap at them over little things. I don’t want to be that parent, you know? I want to be calm and patient, but then I feel so frustrated and it just bursts out. And afterward, I just feel guilty and wonder if I’m damaging our relationship. It's like this cycle I can't break.

I wonder if I’m damaging our relationship

It’s like there’s this constant worry in the back of my mind that I’m messing everything up. I know it’s normal to feel stressed, but sometimes it just feels like too much.

patient.query: It’s hard to pinpoint exactly. I guess part of it is just the pressure I put on myself to be perfect. I want to be the best parent I can be, but then I feel like I’m always falling short. And when things get hectic, it’s like I forget everything I want to do and just react. It’s really frustrating—I just wish I could find a way to break free from that.

automatic_thoughts: I’m always falling short
I forget everything I want to do
I wish I could break free

patient.query: It feels like this constant weight on my shoulders, like I’m always being watched or judged, even if it’s just by myself. There’s this fear of not measuring up—of failing them in some way. And when I think about all the things I should be doing differently, it just spirals. It makes me anxious and sometimes even a bit paralyzed, like I’m afraid to make any decision for fear of it being the “wrong” one.

triggers: thinking about things to do differently
automatic_thoughts: fear of not measuring up
fear of failing
fear of making the wrong decision

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

patient.query: Yeah, it’s like there’s this constant spotlight on me. I feel like every little thing I do is under scrutiny, especially by my kids or even friends and family. It makes me second-guess myself all the time. I worry they’ll notice when I mess up or when I’m not as patient as I wish I could be. It’s exhausting, really. Sometimes, I just want to curl up and hide instead of facing that pressure.

automatic_thoughts: every little thing I do is under scrutiny
I might mess up
I'm not as patient as I wish I could be

patient.query: It really is draining. I think sometimes I just want to feel like I can breathe and be human without that mask of perfection. But then there’s this nagging voice that says I should always strive to do more, be more. It feels like I’m in a tug-of-war with myself, and I don’t know how to find that balance. It’s hard to admit when I’m struggling because I don’t want anyone to think less of me.

automatic_thoughts: I should always strive to do more
I should be more
I don't want anyone to think less of me
